initial_program:
% Count rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% The output cell (same coordinates as input)
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outside = border cells whose colour is not 8, and the region reachable
% through cells that are not 8 (i.e. colour 0 or 2)
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1),
    V != 8.

% Floodfill outward through cells that are not 8
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V),
    V != 8.

% Determine the final colour of each output cell
% 8cells stay 8
output(R, C, 8) :- input(R, C, 8).
% 2cells stay 2
output(R, C, 2) :- input(R, C, 2).
% Zero cells that can reach the outside stay 0
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, 0), outside(R, C).
% Zero cells that are enclosed become colour 3
output(R, C, 3) :- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% Count rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% The output cell (same coordinates as input)
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outside = border cells whose colour is not 8, and the region reachable
% through cells that are not 8 (i.e. colour 0 or 2)
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1),
    V != 8.

% Floodfill outward through cells that are not 8
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V),
    V != 8.

% Determine the final colour of each output cell
% 8cells stay 8
output(R, C, 8) :- input(R, C, 8).
% 2cells stay 2
output(R, C, 2) :- input(R, C, 2).
% Zero cells that can reach the outside stay 0
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, 0), outside(R, C).
% Zero cells that are enclosed become colour 3
output(R, C, 3) :- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% Count rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% The output cell (same coordinates as input)
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outside = border cells whose colour is not 8, and the region reachable
% through cells that are not 8 (i.e. colour 0 or 2)
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1),
    V != 8.

% Floodfill outward through cells that are not 8
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V),
    V != 8.

% Determine the final colour of each output cell
% 8cells stay 8
output(R, C, 8) :- input(R, C, 8).
% 2cells stay 2
output(R, C, 2) :- input(R, C, 2).
% Zero cells that can reach the outside stay 0
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, 0), outside(R, C).
% Zero cells that are enclosed become colour 3
output(R, C, 3) :- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C).

final_program:
% Count rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% The output cell (same coordinates as input)
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% 4connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Outside = border cells whose colour is not 8, and the region reachable
% through cells that are not 8 (i.e. colour 0 or 2)
outside(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    n_rows(N), n_cols(M),
    (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1),
    V != 8.

% Floodfill outward through cells that are not 8
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V),
    V != 8.

% Determine the final colour of each output cell
% 8cells stay 8
output(R, C, 8) :- input(R, C, 8).
% 2cells stay 2
output(R, C, 2) :- input(R, C, 2).
% Zero cells that can reach the outside stay 0
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, 0), outside(R, C).
% Zero cells that are enclosed become colour 3
output(R, C, 3) :- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C).
